title: Tree-Based Models Path
description: Build fluency in Tree-Based Models inside Generative Models by moving from foundational terms into adjacent methods, variants, and extensions.

steps.title: Oversampling
steps.summary: Oversampling belongs to the Generative Models area of AI/ML, within Tree-Based Models. It sits inside Generative Models / Tree-Based Models. Compare it with Holdout Blending and CGAN++. It opens into SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique) and Oversampling Techniques.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: CGAN++
steps.summary: CGAN++ belongs to the Generative Models area of AI/ML, within Tree-Based Models. It sits inside Generative Models / Tree-Based Models. Start after cGAN and R(2+1)D.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Holdout Blending
steps.summary: Holdout Blending belongs to the Generative Models area of AI/ML, within Tree-Based Models. It sits inside Generative Models / Tree-Based Models. Start after Blending and Holdout Method.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Stratified shuffle split
steps.summary: Stratified shuffle split belongs to the Generative Models area of AI/ML, within Tree-Based Models. It sits inside Generative Models / Tree-Based Models. Start after Shuffle split.
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.
